name: Hadanite
description: Hadanite is a pinkish purple colored mineral found in small-scale rock deposits. Some deposits can be mined with a handheld mining tool, while others need at least the power of a size 0 mining laser. It can be found on both planetary surfaces or attached to asteroids. == Description == A crystal oscillator sought out for its extremely high frequency of vibration which makes it useful in various applications. == Harvest locations == == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == Trivia == During PTU Alpha 3.15.0 there was a bug that allow players turn dropped ore into a mining node. == References ==

name: Janalite
description: Janalite is an extremely rare and valuable mineral found in small-scale rock deposits. == Description == == Trade data == == Harvest locations == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Quantanium
description:
Quantanium (Raw) is a volatile mineral that can be mined from asteroids or ore deposits and be refined into quantanium. Raw quantanium  degrades over time and must be deposited at a refinery deck or it will explode violently.

name: Aphorite
description: Aphorite is a mineable commodity with a relatively low instability. == Description == Decorative jewel crystal prized for its multi-hued tones. == Trade data == == Harvest locations == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Dolivine
description: Dolivine is a green colored mineable gemstone. == Description == A dull green gemstone that is used decoratively but also can be used for industrial purposes thanks to its resistance to weathering and high density. == Trade data == == Harvest locations == == Lore == Dolivine came to prominence as a gemstone after Imperator Illyana Messer VI wore a 45 carat dolivine brooch to her niece Astrid’s wedding in 2690. == Gallery == == Development == == See also == Mining == References ==

name: Quantanium (Raw)
description: Quantanium (Raw) is a volatile mineral that can be mined from asteroids or ore deposits and be refined into quantanium. Raw quantanium degrades over time and must be deposited at a refinery deck or it will explode violently. == Description == == Harvest locations == == Gallery == == Development == == See also == Mining Refining Quantanium on Galactapedia == References ==

name: Agricium
description: Agricium is the refined form of agricium ore, it is a tradable commodity. == Description == A rare and valuable silvery metal with a blue-green sheen. Malleable, ductile, and largely non-reactive. == Trade data == == See also == Mining Refining == Development == == References ==

name: Bexalite
description: Bexalite is a mineral commodity. == Description == == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Borase
description: Borase is the refined form of borase ore, it is a tradable commodity. == Description == == Trade data == == See also == Mining Refining == Development == == References ==

name: Laranite
description: Laranite is a gemstone commodity. == Description == A somewhat radioactive, crystalline gemstone. Black with streaks of dark red, it can only be used as jewelry if shielded. == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == Trivia == == References ==

name: Taranite
description: Taranite is a commodity, it is refined from raw taranite. == Description == == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Gold
description: Gold is a metal commodity that is refined from gold ore. == Description == A commonly found ductile metal favored for its conductivity. Though it falls in and out of fashion, it is often used for decorative purposes as well. == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == See also == Mining Refining == References ==

name: Diamond
description: Diamond is a commodity. == Description == An allotrope of the element carbon, diamonds form over billions of years under high heat and pressure. Extremely hard and thermally conductive. == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Titanium
description: Titanium is the refined form of titanium ore, it is a tradable commodity. == Description == This silver-colored chemical element is excellent at producing strong, lightweight alloys. == Trade data == == See also == Mining Refining == Development == == References ==

name: Tungsten
description: Tungsten is the refined form of tungsten ore, it is a tradable commodity. == Description == Used in many different alloys, tungsten in its pure form becomes malleable while maintaining its hardness. == Trade data == == See also == Mining Refining == Development == == References ==

name: Corundum
description: Corundum is a commodity. == Description == The crystalline form of aluminum oxide. Used as a gem. Varieties include ruby, sapphire, and padparadscha. == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Copper
description: Copper is the refined form of coper ore, it is a tradable commodity. == Description == == Trade data == == See also == Mining Refining == Development == == References ==

name: Beryl
description: Beryl is a commodity. == Description == A mineral that creates natural hexagonal crystals. Colorless in its pure form, its impure forms include the gems emerald and aquamarine. == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Hephaestanite
description: Hephaestanite is a mineral commodity. == Description == == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Iron
description: Iron is the refined form of iron ore, it is a tradable commodity. == Description == == Trade data == == Gallery == == See also == Mining Refining == Development == == References ==

name: Quartz
description: Quartz is a mineral commodity. == Description == A chiral crystal composed of silicon and oxygen that can be found in a wide variety of forms and colors. == Trade data == == Gallery == == Development == == References ==

name: Aluminum
description: Aluminum is the refined form of aluminum ore, it is a tradable commodity. == Description == A chemically reactive, gray metal that is malleable, lightweight, ductile, strong, and resistant to corrosion. Capable of superconductivity. == Trade data == == Gallery == == See also == Mining Refining == Development == == References ==
